title: Lottie Animated Framework Icons Spec
body:
# Lottie Animated Framework Icons Spec

## Current State
Four framework cards use unicode characters as visual icons:
- Strengths: ★ — amber (`#f59e0b`)
- 16 Personalities: ▮ — purple (`#6366f1`)
- DISC: ▲ — pink (`#ec4899`)
- Enneagram: ○ — green (`#10b981`)

## Recommendation: Lottie via `lottie-react`

```bash
npm install lottie-react
```

Package size: ~30KB gzip. Renders JSON animation files — no canvas, no WebGL.

## Animation Style Per Framework

| Framework | Icon Concept | Animation | Color |
|-----------|-------------|-----------|-------|
| Strengths | Rising bar chart / upward arrow | Bars grow up on loop, pause 2s | `#f59e0b` amber |
| 16 Personalities | 2×2 grid of dots | Dots pulse outward in sequence | `#6366f1` purple |
| DISC | 4-segment dial/pie | Segments fill clockwise | `#ec4899` pink |
| Enneagram | 9-pointed star / circle with 9 nodes | Nodes light up around circle | `#10b981` green |

All animations: **loop with 2s pause** between cycles (active, not frantic).

## File Specs

- Format: Lottie JSON
- Size: **≤ 15KB per file** (no embedded images, vector shapes only)
- Dimensions: 64×64 viewbox
- Frame rate: 30fps (not 60 — half the data, imperceptible difference)
- Colors: parametric (colorable via `lottie-react` `rendererSettings` or just baked in)

## Loading Behavior

```tsx
import Lottie from 'lottie-react';
import strengthsAnim from '../assets/lottie/strengths.json';

<Lottie
  animationData={strengthsAnim}
  loop={true}
  autoplay={true}
  style={{ width: 64, height: 64 }}
  rendererSettings={{ preserveAspectRatio: 'xMidYMid slice' }}
/>
```

**Graceful degradation:** Wrap in `<Suspense>` with a colored placeholder div (matching framework color) as fallback. If Lottie fails to parse, fallback renders instantly with no layout shift.

## Procurement Options (in priority order)

1. **LottieFiles.com** — search "strengths", "personality", "chart", "circle nodes" — free files exist, check license (CC0 or editorial-use-ok)
2. **Custom SVG→Lottie** — design 4 simple vector shapes in Figma, export via LottieFiles plugin — 2-3 hours design work
3. **CSS animation fallback** — if Lottie procurement is blocked, use CSS `@keyframes` on SVG shapes (still no unicode)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 framework cards show Lottie icons (no unicode ★▮▲○)
- [ ] Each file ≤ 15KB
- [ ] Loop plays with ~2s pause between cycles (not frantic)
- [ ] Colored placeholder renders on Lottie load failure
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion`: set `autoplay={false}` and show static first frame
